county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Cayuga and Seneca Counties in 1817. Home of Cornell University and Ithaca College. Finger Lakes region. Ithaca is county seat. Progressive political and social history.
county_metadata.research_tips: Tompkins County Clerk in Ithaca holds deed records from 1817 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1817 records split between Cayuga and Seneca Counties. Cornell University Library Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections is a major research repository with regional manuscripts, Anti-Slavery Society papers, and family collections. The History Center in Tompkins County has excellent genealogical files, cemetery records, and local newspapers. Ithaca was an Underground Railroad station and African American community records include St. James AME Zion Church (from 1836). The county's Military Tract heritage means early land grants went to Revolutionary War veterans. DeWitt Historical Society collections merged into the History Center. Cayuga Lake steamboat records document transportation families. The Trumansburg and Dryden area historical societies have local records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1817; county clerk Ithaca

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses despite British burning of Kingston in 1777. Most records had been evacuated.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of original 12 counties (1683). Kingston was first capital of New York State. Huguenot settlement in New Paltz. Hudson River and Catskill Mountains. Dutch colonial heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ulster County Clerk in Kingston holds deed records from 1684 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same era. Kingston was burned by the British in 1777 but most county records had been evacuated to Hurley beforehand. The Ulster County Historical Society has genealogical collections. Kingston was briefly the first capital of New York State (1777) and Senate House State Historic Site has records from that period. The Huguenot settlement at New Paltz (1678) is one of the most important in America and the Huguenot Historical Society has extraordinary family records (DuBois, Deyo, Hasbrouck, Bevier, LeFevre, Freer). Dutch Reformed church records in Kingston (from 1659), Hurley, and Marbletown are essential. The D&H Canal Museum in High Falls has canal records. The Woodstock artists colony (1902+) generated community records. Saugerties Historical Society has local records. For African American research, the Sojourner Truth connection to Ulster County is documented at the Sojourner Truth Library at SUNY New Paltz.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1684; county clerk Kingston

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from colonial era.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent from 1684. Survived 1777 burning of Kingston.

Huguenot family records from one of America's earliest settlements. DuBois, Deyo, Hasbrouck families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Washington County in 1813. Lake George and southern Adirondacks. Tourism dominant. Glens Falls is major city. Great Escape amusement park. Paper mill industry.
county_metadata.research_tips: Warren County Clerk in Queensbury (Lake George area) holds deed records from 1813 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1813 records in Washington County. Lake George was a major colonial and Revolutionary War battlefield and Fort William Henry records document military families. The Warren County Historical Society has genealogical files. The Chapman Museum (Glens Falls) has local history collections including paper mill industry records. Glens Falls was an insurance industry and paper mill center. The Finch Pruyn paper company records document workers. Lake George tourism industry generated hotel and boarding house records from the 1800s. The Crandall Public Library in Glens Falls has a strong local history collection. For the Adirondack region, lumber camp records and guide-boat builder records are at the Adirondack Museum. Queensbury was named for Queen Charlotte.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1813; county clerk Queensbury

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Originally Charlotte County (1772), renamed Washington in 1784. First county named for George Washington. Upper Hudson Valley. Fort Edward area critical in colonial wars.
county_metadata.research_tips: Washington County Clerk in Fort Edward holds deed records from 1772 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same era. Originally Charlotte County (renamed 1784), it is the first county in the US named for George Washington. Pre-1772 records in Albany County. The Washington County Historical Society in Fort Edward has genealogical files and military records. The county was a major corridor for colonial wars (French and Indian War, Revolutionary War) and Fort Edward was a key military installation. Rogers Rangers operated from this area. The Jane McCrea story is associated with the county. Scotch-Irish and New England settlers dominated early immigration. Granville and Cambridge have strong agricultural community records. The Battenkill Valley has well-preserved town records. For Vermont connections, many families crossed Lake Champlain and the Vermont border frequently — check Rutland and Bennington County VT records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1772 (as Charlotte County); county clerk Fort Edward

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Ontario and Seneca Counties in 1823. Lake Ontario county. Palmyra was where Joseph Smith founded LDS movement. Erie Canal runs through. Apple farming region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wayne County Clerk in Lyons holds deed records from 1823 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1823 records in Ontario and Seneca Counties. Palmyra is of worldwide genealogical interest as the location where Joseph Smith published the Book of Mormon (1830) and organized the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. The Hill Cumorah Visitors Center and church archives have Smith family and early LDS records. The Wayne County Historical Society in Lyons has genealogical files. The Erie Canal runs through Lyons, Newark, and Palmyra and canal-era records are at NYS Archives. The county's apple and fruit industry generated agricultural records. Sodus Bay on Lake Ontario has maritime and fishing records. The Williamson-Pultneyville Historical Society has lakeshore community records. For the Burned-over District religious movement records (Second Great Awakening), check local church records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1823; county clerk Lyons

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. Records well preserved from colonial period.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of original 12 counties (1683). Northern NYC suburb. White Plains was scene of 1776 battle. Wealthy suburban county. Originally included Bronx before 1874/1914 transfers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Westchester County Clerk in White Plains holds deed records from 1684 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same era. The Westchester County Archives at the Records Center in Elmsford is a major genealogical resource with deed books, naturalization records, court records, and census materials. The Westchester County Historical Society has family files and published local histories. For colonial families, Dutch Reformed and Anglican church records are essential. The Philipsburg Manor records (Philipse family) at Historic Hudson Valley document colonial tenant farming. The Battle of White Plains (1776) records are at NARA. For the African American community, Westchester had both enslaved and free Black populations from the colonial era. Tarrytown Historical Society has Washington Irving-related records. The county originally included what is now the Bronx (transferred 1874-1898) so early records may cover Bronx territory. Yonkers, Mount Vernon, and New Rochelle have separate city-level records and ethnic community archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1684; county clerk White Plains and Westchester Archives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Genesee County in 1841. Western NY agricultural county. Named for the Wyoming Valley (from Lenape word). Warsaw is county seat. Silver Springs and Attica area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wyoming County Clerk in Warsaw holds deed records from 1841 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1841 records in Genesee County. The Wyoming County Historical Society in Warsaw has genealogical files and cemetery records. The county is rural and agricultural with dairy farming dominant. Attica State Prison (1931) generated employee records. The county was settled by New Englanders from Connecticut and Massachusetts via Holland Land Company purchases. The Middlebury Academy records (from 1817) document early educational families. The Wyoming Valley Methodist Conference records cover religious life. Warsaw Salt Springs generated early industrial records. Many county families are connected to the broader western NY settlement pattern from the Holland Purchase area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1841; county clerk Warsaw

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Ontario County in 1823. Finger Lakes region between Keuka and Seneca Lakes. Penn Yan is county seat (named for Pennsylvania + Yankee settlers). Wine country. Mennonite communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Yates County Clerk in Penn Yan holds deed records from 1823 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1823 records in Ontario County. The Yates County Historical Society in Penn Yan has genealogical files and local histories. Penn Yan's unique name reflects its dual settlement by Pennsylvanians (mostly German) and New Englanders (Yankees). The county has significant Mennonite and Hutterite communities with their own record-keeping traditions. Keuka Lake wine industry records document vineyard families. The Jemima Wilkinson community (Universal Friend, 1788-1819) settled in this area and their unusual communal records are at Cornell University and Yates County History Center. Dundee and Benton area records reflect the agricultural Southern Tier character. The Oliver House Museum has Penn Yan family records. For Keuka College and related records check the college archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1823; county clerk Penn Yan
